description:
A model rocket has become critically unstable due to the addition of an aft-mounted GPS tracker. You must restore the rocket's stability margin to a safe level (>= 1.2 calibers) without violating strict geometric constraints. 

Starting file: `/home/ga/Documents/rockets/aft_heavy_rocket.ork`

RULES AND CONSTRAINTS:
1. You must strictly use Fin Sweep to push the Center of Pressure (CP) aft.
2. Do NOT change the Fin Height (must remain at original value for launch rail clearance).
3. Do NOT change the Root Chord (must remain at original value to fit motor section).
4. Do NOT move the fin set's position on the body tube.
5. Do NOT delete or reduce the mass of the 'GPS Tracker'.
6. Do NOT add nose weight or any other mass components to cheat the Center of Gravity forward.

EXPECTED ACTIONS:
1. Open the fin set editor and increase the Sweep length significantly until stability is >= 1.2 calibers.
2. Run the existing flight simulation to verify safe flight dynamics.
3. Save the modified design to `/home/ga/Documents/rockets/stable_swept_rocket.ork`.
4. Write an engineering memo to `/home/ga/Documents/exports/sweep_optimization_memo.txt` detailing the initial vs. final sweep length, the final stability margin, and why increasing sweep improves stability.
